title: Brunoniella neocaledonica
taxonomy: Brunoniella neocaledonica is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Brunoniaceae. This family is a part of the order Lamiales, which is a group of flowering plants that includes mint, lavender, and snapdragons. The order Lamiales is a part of the clade Asterids, which is one of the two main clades in the angiosperm phylogeny. The Asterids clade includes a wide range of flowering plant families, including the Lamiaceae and the Scrophulariaceae. Brunoniella neocaledonica's classification highlights its position among other flowering plants.

intro: Brunoniella neocaledonica, also known as the New Caledonian Brunonia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Brunoniaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and digestive problems, due to its reported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Brunoniella neocaledonica include flavonoids, alkaloids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Brunoniella neocaledonica is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the form of a tincture or a powder for the treatment of various health conditions.
